question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user submit multiple timestamp filters (e.g., timestamp=gte:X&timestamp=lte:Y&timestamp=gte:Z) causing extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to add all of them to whereQuery, resulting in conflicting WHERE conditions that cause the database to return an empty result set, effectively suppressing visibility of all transactions in that range?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user use the BlockViewModel's timestamp.from and timestamp.to fields (nsToSecNs of consensusStart and consensusEnd) returned by getBlocks() to determine the exact nanosecond window in which transactions were gossiped, enabling precise transaction timing correlation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a 64-character hex string that is a valid ETH hash format but corresponds to only the first 32 bytes of a 48-byte Hedera hash, causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to match via LIKE prefix and return a block, leaking the full 96-character hash and transaction count of a block the attacker only partially identified?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user use the gas_used field in BlockViewModel to infer the computational complexity of transactions gossiped in a block, correlating high gas_used values with specific smart contract transactions and thereby deanonymizing transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: generateNextLink()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that generateNextLink() uses last(blocks).index as the pagination anchor, meaning if the last block in a page has a non-sequential index (due to gaps), the next link will skip blocks containing transactions, causing transaction gossip data to be silently omitted from paginated enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp filter with operator eq and a specific nanosecond timestamp to query for blocks whose consensus_end exactly matches that timestamp, confirming whether a transaction was finalized at that exact moment, enabling precise transaction gossip timing confirmation?
